text:
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

================================================================================
PATIENT: Raymond Kowalski
MRN: [REDACTED]
DATE OF PROCEDURE: [PENDING PROGRAMMATIC INSERTION]
DATE OF REPORT: [PENDING PROGRAMMATIC INSERTION]
ATTENDING PATHOLOGIST: [Attending Pathologist on Record]
CLINICAL SERVICE: Urology / Urologic Oncology
ACCESSION NUMBER: SP-[REDACTED]
================================================================================

CLINICAL HISTORY:
Seventy-one-year-old male (age at diagnosis: 68 years) presenting with a markedly elevated PSA of 187.4 ng/mL, progressive lower urinary tract symptoms, and new-onset axial back pain. Digital rectal examination revealed a firm, nodular, asymmetric prostate gland with suspected extracapsular extension. Transrectal ultrasound (TRUS) demonstrated an enlarged prostate (estimated volume approximately 65 cc) with hypoechoic lesion(s) in the right peripheral zone extending toward the apex. TRUS-guided systematic 12-core needle biopsy performed by the urology service.

CLINICAL INDICATION: Elevated PSA, suspicious prostate on digital rectal examination and TRUS. Rule out prostate carcinoma.

================================================================================
SPECIMEN(S) SUBMITTED:

Core 1:  Right apex (RA)
Core 2:  Right apex lateral (RAL)
Core 3:  Right mid lateral (RML)
Core 4:  Right mid medial (RMM)
Core 5:  Right base lateral (RBL)
Core 6:  Right base medial (RBM)
Core 7:  Left apex (LA)
Core 8:  Left apex lateral (LAL)
Core 9:  Left mid lateral (LML)
Core 10: Left mid medial (LMM)
Core 11: Left base lateral (LBL)
Core 12: Left base medial (LBM)

All cores received in formalin-fixed, individually labeled cassettes. Cores range in length from 0.9 cm to 1.5 cm. Specimens processed per standard protocol for prostatic needle core biopsies.

================================================================================
GROSS DESCRIPTION:

Received are 12 individually labeled containers, each containing one to two pale tan-pink needle core fragments in formalin. The cores are firm to soft in consistency. Core lengths and calibers are as follows:

  Core 1  (RA):   1.4 cm; 0.2 cm diameter
  Core 2  (RAL):  1.3 cm; 0.2 cm diameter
  Core 3  (RML):  1.5 cm; 0.2 cm diameter
  Core 4  (RMM):  1.4 cm; 0.2 cm diameter
  Core 5  (RBL):  1.2 cm; 0.2 cm diameter
  Core 6  (RBM):  0.9 cm; 0.2 cm diameter
  Core 7  (LA):   1.3 cm; 0.2 cm diameter
  Core 8  (LAL):  1.5 cm; 0.2 cm diameter
  Core 9  (LML):  1.4 cm; 0.2 cm diameter
  Core 10 (LMM):  1.3 cm; 0.2 cm diameter
  Core 11 (LBL):  1.1 cm; 0.2 cm diameter
  Core 12 (LBM):  0.9 cm; 0.2 cm diameter

All cores entirely submitted in labeled cassettes; levels cut at 3 planes per block with H&E stain. No gross lesion is specifically identifiable. No seminal vesicle tissue is grossly apparent in any submitted container.

Representative sections submitted: 12 cassettes (one per core), entirely submitted.

================================================================================
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:

Microscopic examination of all 12 cores reveals the following:

CORES POSITIVE FOR CARCINOMA (10 of 12 cores):

  Core 1 (RA):   Involved. Prostatic adenocarcinoma comprising approximately 95% of core length. Cribriform architecture with solid sheets of highly atypical cells; necrotic intraluminal debris (comedonecrosis) present focally. Gleason pattern 5 predominant with secondary pattern 4 (poorly-formed/fused glands). Perineural invasion identified. Extracapsular extension not assessable on needle biopsy.

  Core 2 (RAL):  Involved. Prostatic adenocarcinoma comprising approximately 80% of core length. Predominantly Gleason pattern 5 (solid nests, single-file infiltrating cells). Secondary Gleason pattern 4 present (poorly-formed glands). No intraluminal crystalloids. Perineural invasion present.

  Core 3 (RML):  Involved. Prostatic adenocarcinoma comprising approximately 70% of core length. Gleason pattern 5 with areas of Gleason pattern 4 (cribriform glands). High nuclear grade; prominent nucleoli. Mitotic figures present (2/high power field). No associated high-grade PIN (obscured by carcinoma).

  Core 4 (RMM):  Involved. Prostatic adenocarcinoma comprising approximately 60% of core length. Mixed Gleason pattern 5 and 4. Focal lymphovascular space invasion identified (endothelium-lined space containing single tumor cells/emboli; confirmed on IHC - see Ancillary Studies).

  Core 5 (RBL):  Involved. Prostatic adenocarcinoma comprising approximately 50% of core length. Gleason pattern 5 (single infiltrating cells and cords) with secondary Gleason pattern 4. Reactive stromal changes (desmoplasia) present surrounding neoplastic glands.

  Core 6 (RBM):  Involved. Prostatic adenocarcinoma comprising approximately 45% of core length. Gleason pattern 5, predominantly solid nests. Secondary pattern 4 poorly formed glands. No benign prostate tissue adjacent.

  Core 7 (LA):   Involved. Prostatic adenocarcinoma comprising approximately 30% of core length. Predominantly Gleason pattern 4 (cribriform and fused glands) with secondary Gleason pattern 5. Residual benign glandular architecture present at periphery of core.

  Core 8 (LAL):  Involved. Prostatic adenocarcinoma comprising approximately 25% of core length. Gleason pattern 4 predominant (poorly-formed/fused glands and cribriform glands). Secondary pattern 5 present (individual cells and small nests infiltrating stroma). Chronic inflammation noted in adjacent benign tissue.

  Core 9 (LML):  Involved. Prostatic adenocarcinoma comprising approximately 20% of core length. Gleason pattern 4 (fused glands, cribriform architecture). Small foci suspicious for Gleason pattern 5 (single infiltrating cells). Stroma shows mild reactive changes.

  Core 10 (LMM): Involved. Prostatic adenocarcinoma comprising approximately 15% of core length. Gleason pattern 4 (poorly-formed glands and cribriform structures). No perineural invasion in this core. Associated high-grade prostatic intraepithelial neoplasia (HGPIN) present in adjacent benign tissue.

CORES NEGATIVE FOR CARCINOMA (2 of 12 cores):

  Core 11 (LBL): Negative for malignancy. Benign prostatic tissue with mild chronic inflammation. Benign prostatic hyperplasia (glandular and fibromuscular). No PIN.

  Core 12 (LBM): Negative for malignancy. Benign prostatic glands and stroma. Mild chronic prostatitis. High-grade PIN present (2 foci, tufting pattern), not qualifying for carcinoma. No malignant cells identified.

================================================================================
IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (ANCILLARY STUDIES):

IHC performed on Core 1 (RA) and Core 4 (RMM) for confirmation of prostatic primary and assessment of lymphovascular invasion:

  PSA (Prostate-Specific Antigen):       POSITIVE (strong, diffuse cytoplasmic)
  PSAP (Prostatic Acid Phosphatase):     POSITIVE (moderate, diffuse cytoplasmic)
  NKX3.1 (Prostate-specific transcription factor): POSITIVE (strong nuclear)
  p63 (Basal cell marker):               NEGATIVE (absence of basal cell layer confirmed)
  HMWCK / CK5/6 (High-molecular-weight cytokeratin, basal cell marker): NEGATIVE
  CD31 (Endothelial marker, Core 4 RMM): POSITIVE highlighting endothelium; tumor cells within CD31-positive vascular channels confirm lymphovascular invasion.
  D2-40 (Podoplanin, lymphatic marker):  POSITIVE in subset of involved vascular channels in Core 4, supporting lymphovascular invasion.

Interpretation: Immunoprofile is consistent with prostatic primary adenocarcinoma. Absence of basal cell markers (p63, HMWCK) in the neoplastic glands confirms invasive adenocarcinoma rather than high-grade PIN or mimicker. Lymphovascular invasion confirmed by IHC.

ADDITIONAL ANCILLARY STUDIES (PENDING):
  - Genomic/molecular testing (somatic NGS panel): Specimen reserved; referral to be placed per clinical team discretion.
  - Germline genetic testing: Not performed on this specimen; to be ordered separately (peripheral blood).

================================================================================
DIAGNOSIS:

1. RIGHT APEX (Core 1, RA):
   PROSTATIC ADENOCARCINOMA, Gleason grade 5 + 4 = 9, Grade Group 5.
   - Extent: 95% of core (1.3/1.4 cm)
   - Perineural invasion: PRESENT
   - Lymphovascular invasion: Not identified in this core

2. RIGHT APEX LATERAL (Core 2, RAL):
   PROSTATIC ADENOCARCINOMA, Gleason grade 5 + 4 = 9, Grade Group 5.
   - Extent: 80% of core (1.0/1.3 cm)
   - Perineural invasion: PRESENT

3. RIGHT MID LATERAL (Core 3, RML):
   PROSTATIC ADENOCARCINOMA, Gleason grade 5 + 4 = 9, Grade Group 5.
   - Extent: 70% of core (1.05/1.5 cm)
   - Perineural invasion: Not identified in this core

4. RIGHT MID MEDIAL (Core 4, RMM):
   PROSTATIC ADENOCARCINOMA, Gleason grade 5 + 4 = 9, Grade Group 5.
   - Extent: 60% of core (0.84/1.4 cm)
   - Lymphovascular invasion: PRESENT (confirmed by IHC, see Ancillary Studies)

5. RIGHT BASE LATERAL (Core 5, RBL):
   PROSTATIC ADENOCARCINOMA, Gleason grade 5 + 4 = 9, Grade Group 5.
   - Extent: 50% of core (0.6/1.2 cm)

6. RIGHT BASE MEDIAL (Core 6, RBM):
   PROSTATIC ADENOCARCINOMA, Gleason grade 5 + 4 = 9, Grade Group 5.
   - Extent: 45% of core (0.4/0.9 cm)

7. LEFT APEX (Core 7, LA):
   PROSTATIC ADENOCARCINOMA, Gleason grade 4 + 5 = 9, Grade Group 5.
   - Extent: 30% of core (0.39/1.3 cm)

8. LEFT APEX LATERAL (Core 8, LAL):
   PROSTATIC ADENOCARCINOMA, Gleason grade 4 + 5 = 9, Grade Group 5.
   - Extent: 25% of core (0.38/1.5 cm)

9. LEFT MID LATERAL (Core 9, LML):
   PROSTATIC ADENOCARCINOMA, Gleason grade 4 + 5 = 9, Grade Group 5.
   - Extent: 20% of core (0.28/1.4 cm)

10. LEFT MID MEDIAL (Core 10, LMM):
    PROSTATIC ADENOCARCINOMA, Gleason grade 4 + 4 = 8, Grade Group 4.
    Note: Minority component of Gleason pattern 5 present but pattern 4 predominant in this core.
    - Extent: 15% of core (0.2/1.3 cm)
    - Associated HGPIN: PRESENT in adjacent benign tissue

11. LEFT BASE LATERAL (Core 11, LBL):
    NEGATIVE FOR MALIGNANCY.
    Benign prostatic tissue with mild chronic inflammation and benign prostatic hyperplasia.

12. LEFT BASE MEDIAL (Core 12, LBM):
    NEGATIVE FOR MALIGNANCY.
    Benign prostatic glands and stroma. High-grade prostatic intraepithelial neoplasia (HGPIN) present.
    Mild chronic prostatitis.

================================================================================
SUMMARY:

  DIAGNOSIS:                     Prostatic adenocarcinoma
  GLEASON SCORE (overall):       9 (5+4), representing the highest grade and most clinically relevant cores
  GRADE GROUP:                   5 (Grade Group 5, per 2014 ISUP/2016 WHO criteria)
  CORES POSITIVE:                10 of 12
  GREATEST PERCENTAGE CORE INVOLVEMENT: 95% (Core 1, Right Apex)
  TOTAL LINEAR LENGTH INVOLVED:  Approximately 6.14 cm of 15.5 cm total core length submitted
  PERINEURAL INVASION:           PRESENT
  LYMPHOVASCULAR INVASION:       PRESENT (confirmed by IHC)
  SEMINAL VESICLE INVOLVEMENT:   Not assessable on needle biopsy (no seminal vesicle tissue present in submitted cores)
  PATHOLOGIC STAGE (biopsy):     pT2 (clinical staging T3b based on concurrent imaging findings)

================================================================================
NOTE:
The Gleason grading has been performed per the 2014 International Society of Urological Pathology (ISUP) consensus guidelines as adopted in the 2016 WHO Classification of Tumours of the Urinary System and Male Genital Organs. The overall Gleason score is assigned based on the highest-grade tumor present across all positive cores; the primary grade (5) reflects the predominant pattern in the highest-volume cores. The overall Grade Group is 5, corresponding to the most adverse prognostic category.

Correlation with clinical, radiographic, and laboratory findings is recommended. The findings in this report are consistent with the clinical staging of T3bN1M1b metastatic prostatic adenocarcinoma (Stage IVB, AJCC 8th edition) as documented in the clinical record.

================================================================================
Electronically signed by: [Attending Pathologist]
Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine
[Institution Name]
[Date: PENDING PROGRAMMATIC INSERTION]
================================================================================
